note: Three linked pieces (Erik 2026-07-07): (1) BASELINE DEFENSE — every character can defend itself, no build required; (2) MARTIAL BACKGROUNDS — warrior paths exist across ALL the axes: a chaos-warrior and an order-warrior share the same basic martial functions (strike/shield/move) expressed utterly differently through their Reach; (3) RACIAL/FORM KITS — a body brings its own weapons: an Ent fights with branch-clubs and root-work. Access model included. Extends universal_roles (Warrior role) + skill_functions (strike/shield by domain). Authored by Aevi (PO).

baselineDefense.principle: No character is helpless. Every character gets a BASELINE DEFENSE KIT free at creation — not a class, a floor: the universal animal competence of protecting yourself. Small numbers, always available, never gated.

kit.name: Brace
kit.grants: Guard, block, dodge, cover — reduce incoming harm in a beat. Everyone can raise their arms.
kit.cannot: Not armor; a Tier-III strike still hurts through it.

kit.name: Strike
kit.grants: Hit with what you have — fist, staff, stone, branch. Modest, honest damage.
kit.cannot: Untrained force; the Edge's economy it is not.

kit.name: Break Away
kit.grants: Disengage, flee, put distance or an obstacle between you and harm.
kit.cannot: The faster or the prepared can still catch you.

kit.name: Raise the Alarm
kit.grants: Call for help, alert, rally whoever's near — defense by numbers.
kit.cannot: Someone must be near to hear.

baselineDefense.formModifier: The kit expresses through the FORM: a human's Strike is a fist or staff; an ENT's Strike is a BRANCH-CLUB (a limb swung like a beam) and its Brace is BARKSKIN (the body IS armor); a Blocklands-native braces by raising a quick wall. Form-kits below.
baselineDefense.engineNote: Baseline kit = 4 free zero-cost abilities at creation, powerSystem 'baseline', excluded from skill-point costs and caps. SNG-022 reconciliation grants them to existing characters on login (your Ent gets its branch-club next login).

formKits.note: A body brings its own weapons and defenses — form-kits are small racial/physiology ability sets granted by ORIGIN FORM, on top of baseline. Manifest-race characters especially.

kits.name: The Grove's Arms

grants.name: Branch-Club
grants.desc: A limb swung like a falling beam — slow, enormous, honest. The Ent's Strike, writ in timber.
grants.cannot: Slow — the quick sidestep it; fire answers it terribly.

grants.name: Barkskin
grants.desc: The body is armor — blows land on bark and heartwood. Passive toughness + an active brace that shrugs ordinary strikes.
grants.cannot: Fire and axe bite deep; bark regrows slow.

grants.name: Root-Hold
grants.desc: Roots driven into earth — immovable stance, or roots snared around a foe's legs. Bind and stand, both from below.
grants.cannot: Needs earth (stone floors and city pavement resist); rooted means not moving.

grants.name: Root-Reach
grants.desc: Feel through the ground along root-lines — what walks nearby, where water runs; in living earth, a slow tunneling reach.
grants.cannot: Range is soil-bound; dead ground is silent.

kits.drift: Form abilities carry the life/spiritual disposition; heavy use deepens the Ent's green nature.

grants.name: Quick Wall
grants.desc: Snap a barrier together from whatever's near — the block-grammar's defense reflex, working (weakly) even outside the domain.
grants.cannot: Outside the Blocklands it's improvised cover, not true block-work.

kits.name: —
kits.note: Humans and unlisted forms use the plain baseline kit; their depth comes from training (backgrounds below).

martialBackgrounds.principle: Erik: 'A chaos warrior and an order warrior would be very different, but the same basic kind of skills — expressed novelly through their own reach combos.' Exactly. A MARTIAL BACKGROUND = the Warrior role + a Reach = a distinct fighting tradition. Same functions (strike/shield/move/break), utterly different expression. Each background grants: its Reach's martial-facing abilities as HOME tradition + a signature COMBO (the cross-domain braid that defines its style) + a defense identity.

backgrounds.name: Marcher (violence+order)
backgrounds.style: The formal duelist — the Edge by the forms; economical, decisive, honorable.
backgrounds.signatureCombo: the_edge + latticework → 'The Perfect Form' (a fight conducted like a proof — every strike lawful, every opening foreseen)
backgrounds.defenseIdentity: discipline — the form IS the armor

backgrounds.name: Churn-Reaver (violence+chaos)
backgrounds.style: The chaos-warrior — improvised weapons, broken footing, wins by the fight coming apart. Same strike/shield functions as the Marcher, nothing alike in expression.
backgrounds.signatureCombo: wildcraft + strike_basic → 'The Unraveling Blow' (a strike that lands where the plan broke — damage scales with the disorder you made)
backgrounds.defenseIdentity: unpredictability — never where the blow lands

backgrounds.name: Temple Guardian (body+peace)
backgrounds.style: The protector — Soma-mastery held as devotion; will not start, will absolutely finish.
backgrounds.signatureCombo: soma + stillcraft → 'The Immovable Calm' (a guard that de-escalates — attackers strike, are absorbed, and somehow the fight is over)
backgrounds.defenseIdentity: the trained body as sanctuary — yours and others'

backgrounds.name: War-Thinker (mind+order)
backgrounds.style: The Cogitant fighter — never lifts a blade; the battlefield is a model and you are already checkmated.
backgrounds.signatureCombo: noesis + latticework → 'The Foregone Conclusion' (out-model an opponent so completely their attack fails before it starts)
backgrounds.defenseIdentity: prediction — harm addressed before it exists

backgrounds.name: Umbral Blade (violence+dark)
backgrounds.style: The unseen fighter — war as a thing that happens in the dark; one strike, from nowhere, finished.
backgrounds.signatureCombo: umbracraft + the_edge → 'The Unseen Cut' (already authored — the braid IS this background's signature)
backgrounds.defenseIdentity: absence — you cannot hit what was never there

backgrounds.name: Thorn-Warden (life+violence)
backgrounds.style: The grove's answer to the axe — living weapons, thorn and root and regrowth; the Ent's natural background. Fights so that things may keep living.
backgrounds.signatureCombo: root_hold + quickening → 'The Grasping Green' (roots bind while living growth mends you mid-fight — attrition the green always wins)
backgrounds.defenseIdentity: regrowth — wound the warden, the warden heals; wound the grove, the grove remembers

backgrounds.name: Blaze-Lance (light+violence)
backgrounds.style: The revealing strike — light as a weapon; nothing hidden, nothing spared.
backgrounds.signatureCombo: radiance + kindle → 'The Judging Beam' (a strike that reveals as it burns — illusions die first, then the target's cover)
backgrounds.defenseIdentity: exposure — an enemy seen truly is half-beaten

backgrounds.name: Pale Reaper (death+peace)
backgrounds.style: The mercy that fights — an Ashwarden militant; ends what must end, cleanly, without hate. The most feared and gentlest style.
backgrounds.signatureCombo: palework + stillcraft → 'The Quiet End' (a strike that concludes — against the dying, the raging, the unnatural, it simply finishes what was already ending)
backgrounds.defenseIdentity: acceptance — fear of the end is the opening; the Reaper has none

martialBackgrounds.designNote: Eight backgrounds from six Reaches — the axes multiply: violence crossed with order/chaos/dark/light/life/death yields distinct traditions, and body/mind yields two more without 'violence' at all. Any Reach pair with a strike-or-shield function makes a martial background. Generation (SNG-020) can author MORE backgrounds by the same grammar: Warrior role + Reach pair + signature braid.

access.atCreation: Baseline kit: free, everyone. Form-kit: free with the form (an Ent is born with branches). Martial background: choose at creation as your ROLE+HOMELAND (SNG-017 origins) — grants the home Reach's martial abilities at base levelReq + the signature combo as a KNOWN technique.
access.inPlay: Any background is learnable later through fiction — a teacher of that tradition, training at its home place (the Redline teaches Marcher; the Flesh-Temple teaches Guardian), or a quest — via character.traditionAccess (the generalized precursorAccess pattern from the SNG-017 addendum). The signature combo is discoverable by use like any braid (SNG-026).
access.entAnswer: Erik's Ent, concretely: (1) next login, SNG-022 grants the baseline kit — it can Brace/Strike/Break Away NOW, expressed as timber and bark; (2) the ent form-kit gives Branch-Club, Barkskin, Root-Hold, Root-Reach — the club-branches and root-based skills asked for; (3) Thorn-Warden is its natural martial background, learnable from the Deepwood's Walkers or claimable at creation for new Ents; (4) Harmonic remains learnable at the Heights per the gating fix. The Ent is never again a powerful being that can't defend itself.

buildImplication: Content DONE here: baseline kit (4), ent form-kit (4), blocklands reflex (1), 8 martial backgrounds w/ signature combos. Engine (fold into SNG-017 + SNG-022 + SNG-026): (a) grant baseline at creation + reconcile-grant to existing characters; (b) form-kit granted by origin form; (c) background = origins.json entry granting homeReach abilities + signature combo; (d) new combos (Perfect Form, Unraveling Blow, Immovable Calm, Foregone Conclusion, Grasping Green, Judging Beam, Quiet End) added to combination_recipes as discoverable. No new systems — composition of existing ones.
